skill.name: cost-optimization
skill.description: Optimize cloud costs through resource rightsizing, tagging strategies, reserved instances, and spending analysis. Use when reducing cloud expenses, analyzing infrastructure costs, or implementing cost governance across AWS, Azure, and GCP.
skill.summary: Optimize cloud costs through resource rightsizing, tagging strategies, reserved instances, and spending analysis across AWS, Azure, and GCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate cloud cost optimization guide containing documentation and Terraform configuration examples. All 30 static findings are false positives: backtick code blocks in markdown were misidentified as shell commands, AWS service names (EC2, Fargate, Lambda) were misidentified as C2 keywords, and infrastructure configurations were misidentified as system reconnaissance. No actual code execution, malware, or security threats present.

high_findings.title: External Commands Detection (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at 23 locations. These are markdown code fences (```hcl, ```) containing Terraform configuration examples, not actual shell commands. The skill contains no code execution.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: All detected locations are markdown code blocks showing Terraform HCL configurations. No actual backtick execution or shell commands present in the skill.

high_findings.title: C2 Keywords Detection (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'C2 keywords' at lines 75, 76, 247. These are legitimate AWS service names: EC2, Fargate, Lambda. Not command-and-control malware indicators.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected terms are standard AWS service names used in cloud cost optimization context, not malware C2 indicators.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at line 3. No cryptographic code exists in this skill - only documentation text.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains only description text about cloud cost optimization. No cryptographic algorithms present.

high_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detection (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'System reconnaissance' at lines 91, 94, 122. These are references to S3 lifecycle configurations and Azure Hybrid Benefit - legitimate cloud resource configurations, not reconnaissance.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The references are to cloud infrastructure configurations (S3 lifecycle rules, Azure Hybrid Benefit), not system reconnaissance tools or techniques.

content.value_statement: Reduce cloud spending across AWS, Azure, and GCP through resource rightsizing, reserved instances, and cost governance strategies.
actual_capabilities: Provide AWS cost optimization strategies including Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, and Spot Instances
Recommend Azure cost management approaches using Reserved VM Instances and Hybrid Benefit
Suggest GCP committed use discounts and sustained use optimizations
Guide implementation of cost allocation tagging strategies
Create Terraform configurations for S3 lifecycle policies and auto-scaling
limitations: Does not execute actual cloud API calls or modify resources
Cannot access real cloud account billing data
Does not provide real-time cost monitoring
Examples are illustrative and require customization for production use

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Reducing Cloud Bills
use_cases.description: Analyze current infrastructure and recommend rightsizing, reserved instances, and auto-scaling configurations to reduce monthly cloud spending by 30-50%.

use_cases.title: Finance Team Budget Management
use_cases.description: Implement cost allocation tags, budget alerts, and spending dashboards to provide visibility and governance over cloud expenditures.

use_cases.title: Multi-Cloud Strategy Optimization
use_cases.description: Evaluate and optimize costs across AWS, Azure, and GCP using committed use discounts, spot instances, and managed services.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Cost Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my cloud infrastructure for cost optimization opportunities. We use [AWS/Azure/GCP] and spend approximately $[amount] per month. What are the top 5 ways to reduce our cloud costs?
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with cloud cost optimization

prompt_templates.title: Reserved Instance Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me develop a Reserved Instance strategy for our AWS production workloads. We have 50 EC2 instances running 24/7 with consistent utilization. Which instance types should we consider and what commitment level makes sense?

prompt_templates.title: Tagging Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a comprehensive tagging strategy for our cloud resources. Include recommended tag keys, values, and enforcement mechanisms. We need to track costs by environment, team, and application.

prompt_templates.title: Storage Cost Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Optimize our cloud storage costs. We have approximately 10TB of data in S3 that is accessed infrequently after 30 days. Design a lifecycle policy and recommend appropriate storage classes.

output_examples.input: How can I reduce my AWS monthly bill?
output_examples.output:
Here are the top cost optimization strategies for AWS:

1. **Reserved Instances**: Commit to 1-3 year terms for steady-state workloads. Save 30-72% vs On-Demand.

2. **Savings Plans**: More flexible than RIs. Compute Savings Plans offer 66% savings across EC2, Fargate, Lambda.

3. **Spot Instances**: Use for batch jobs, CI/CD, stateless workloads. Save up to 90%.

4. **Right-Sizing**: Analyze with AWS Compute Optimizer. Downsize over-provisioned instances.

5. **Delete Unused Resources**: Remove idle EBS volumes, unattached EIPs, old snapshots.

6. **S3 Lifecycle Policies**: Move older data to STANDARD_IA after 30 days, Glacier after 90 days.

7. **Cost Allocation Tags**: Enable tracking by team, project, environment.

output_examples.input: What is the best Azure cost optimization approach?
output_examples.output:
Key Azure cost optimization strategies:

1. **Reserved VM Instances**: Save up to 72% with 1-3 year commitments. Flexible sizing and exchangeable.

2. **Azure Hybrid Benefit**: Use existing Windows Server licenses. Save up to 80% with RIs.

3. **Spot VMs**: For dev/test and interruptible workloads. Save up to 90%.

4. **Azure Advisor**: Review recommendations for right-sizing, deleted resources, and storage optimization.

5. **Azure Cost Management**: Set budgets, configure alerts at 80% threshold, use anomaly detection.

6. **Managed Services**: Use Azure SQL, App Service, Functions instead of VMs where possible.

best_practices: Start with visibility: Implement cost allocation tags before optimizing
Use a tiered approach: Reserved capacity for baseline, spot for variable workloads
Review costs weekly: Set up budget alerts at 80% threshold
Automate cleanup: Delete unused resources automatically
anti_patterns: Using On-Demand instances for predictable steady-state workloads
Not tagging resources - losing visibility into cost attribution
Ignoring storage lifecycle policies - paying for expensive hot storage

faq.question: Does this skill execute changes in my cloud account?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance, recommendations, and Terraform configuration examples. It does not make any actual API calls or modify your cloud resources.

faq.question: Which cloud providers does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill covers AWS, Azure, and GCP. It provides provider-specific strategies for Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, and cost management tools.

faq.question: How much can I save with these optimizations?
faq.answer: Savings vary by workload. Typical savings range from 30-50% for general workloads, up to 70-90% for interruptible batch jobs using spot instances.

faq.question: Do I need special permissions to implement these recommendations?
faq.answer: Yes. You will need appropriate IAM permissions for your cloud provider. For AWS: Cost Explorer, Compute Optimizer read access. For Azure: Cost Management reader. For GCP: Billing viewer.

faq.question: Can this skill help with multi-cloud cost optimization?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill covers strategies for each provider and includes guidance on multi-cloud cost governance and comparison.

faq.question: What tools does this skill recommend for cost monitoring?
faq.answer: Provider-native tools: AWS Cost Explorer, Azure Cost Management, GCP Recommender. Third-party: CloudHealth, CloudAbility, Kubecost for Kubernetes.
